summary: Spolsky describes his experience using TextMate with Markdown on a MacBook Pro to compose long blog articles, since his usual tool CityDesk is Windows-only. He found the combination surprisingly pleasant for generating clean HTML with curly quotes. However, he criticizes OS X's font antialiasing as inferior to Windows ClearType and reports severe system freezes triggered by WiFi disconnections. He ultimately concludes that Word 2007 on Windows XP remains the superior writing experience.
key_points: TextMate with Markdown mode provides a smooth writing experience on Mac with clean HTML output and readable source
OS X font antialiasing with monospaced fonts was noticeably worse than Windows ClearType at the time
Mac OS X had a critical stability issue where brief WiFi drops caused complete system freezes requiring hard reboots every two hours
Despite trying Mac alternatives, Word 2007 on Windows XP was still the better overall writing experience due to autocorrection and display quality
money_quotes: The combination I found that made me happiest was TextMate in Markdown mode. It was a surprisingly good experience.
Even Markdown source is quite clean and still highly readable, useful if you need to post the same content to Usenet or use it in plain text somewhere.
I don't understand all these people who say that Macs never crash.
On Windows, it just comes back, while on the Mac, I get a spinning colored ball and everything is frozen. Everything. Forever.
If I try to wait it out the beachball will still be spinning the next morning.
It was like a Windows 3.1 deja vu all over again thing.
key_insight: Even beloved tools have rough edges — Spolsky's pragmatic evaluation of Mac writing tools in 2006 challenged the prevailing narrative of Mac superiority while giving early recognition to Markdown as a clean authoring format.
